message:
fix(node): NaN/Inf guards in BOCPD models and push_bounded for remaining unbounded collections

Hardens several connector modules against non-finite floating-point
propagation and unbounded memory growth.

bocpd.rs:
- student_t_pdf(): guard against sigma_sq <= 0 and nu <= 0 returning
  floor value 1e-300 instead of NaN/Inf; also clamp non-finite exp()
  results to 1e-300
- neg_binomial_pmf(): guard against p <= 0, p >= 1, and r <= 0; clamp
  non-finite exp() results
- CategoricalModel::predictive_prob(): use fold instead of sum() for
  count accumulation; guard total <= 0

operator_intelligence.rs:
- Add MAX_MISSING_SOURCES = 256 bound
- mark_source_unavailable() uses push_bounded instead of unbounded push

policy_checkpoint.rs:
- Add MAX_CHECKPOINTS = 4096 bound
- Both checkpoint creation paths use push_bounded

supervision.rs:
- restart_timestamps uses push_bounded with MAX_EVENTS instead of
  unbounded push

vef_claim_integration.rs:
- Add MAX_GATE_RESULTS = 4096 and MAX_SCOREBOARD = 4096 bounds
- gate_results and scoreboard use push_bounded

repro_bundle_export.rs:
- Replace unwrap() with expect() for push_bounded guarantee

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
